title: Only test apparmor if kernel support is detected
body: In containerized tests running on Ubuntu, we are running the apparmor tests unconditionally, even when they're running on a kernel with no support. Gate them on kernel support being found in /sys.

comments.body: @vrothberg PTAL, want to make sure this doesn't break actual apparmor testing.

:pushpin: Commit ddd2df6 has been approved by `baude`

<!-- @rh-atomic-bot r=baude ddd2df6d3081309db62d7272a03612027f31b5fa -->
I think that's something we need to fix in the code then (i.e., at runtime rather than at build time). Currently, AppArmor support is disabled when the `TRAVIS` env variable is set.
I won't have time to check that now, but will have a look over the weekend.

:sunny: Test successful - [status-papr](https://s3.amazonaws.com/aos-ci/ghprb/projectatomic/libpod/97aca4b5d8cb2b09ac86953dee435af362ec8f7e.1532109825280221952/index.html)
Approved by: baude
Pushing 97aca4b5d8cb2b09ac86953dee435af362ec8f7e to master...
:eyes: Test was successful, but fast-forwarding failed: 422 Update is not a fast forward

A NACK on this PR from me as I prefer to make those checks at runtime. Otherwise, we will run into regressions. I opened a PR for the runtime checks (see https://github.com/projectatomic/libpod/pull/1128). @mheon @baude @rhatdan PTAL.
I'm fine deprecating in favor of #1128
